hero.title: Young Professional Visa Program — Switzerland
hero.description: We guide qualified non-EU young professionals through every step of the YPV Switzerland application process — helping you navigate it with clarity and confidence.
hero.disclaimer: Free pre-screening · Takes 3 minutes · No commitment required
hero.subtitle: Pre-Screening Form

problem.title: The Swiss YPV — Real, But Easy to Get Wrong
problem.body1: Most non-European applicants face challenges due to unclear requirements, incomplete documentation, lack of awareness about hiring periods, and misunderstandings about language requirements that are not actually part of the visa process. Even a small mistake can result in significant delays or rejection.
problem.body2: This webinar is based on real experience, guiding you through the exact steps that lead to approval — tailored for applicants from eligible non-European countries.

what_is_ypv.title: What is the Young Professional Visa?
what_is_ypv.body: The Young Professional Visa (YPV) Switzerland is a government-supported trainee exchange program that allows young professionals from selected non-European countries to gain relevant work experience in Switzerland through a simplified work permit process. It is designed for professional development rather than general employment, meaning applicants must have a job offer related to their field of study. Unlike standard work permits, the process is more accessible due to bilateral agreements between Switzerland and certain countries, and it is not strictly dependent on the local labor market situation.
what_is_ypv.disclaimer: This is general information for educational purposes. Always verify current requirements with the Swiss embassy or State Secretariat for Migration (SEM) before applying.

countries.title: Eligible Countries
countries.lead: The Young Professional Visa is available to citizens of these 15 countries through bilateral agreements with Switzerland:
countries.note: Special age limits apply for Australia (20–30), New Zealand (18–30), and Russia (18–30). All others: 18–35.

host.title: Your Guide Through the Process
host.subtitle: Experience-based guidance for the YPV Switzerland application process
host.bio: This guidance is based on real experience helping young professionals from non-European countries navigate the Swiss Young Professional Visa process. We understand the common pitfalls, required paperwork, and what Swiss authorities look for — so you don't have to figure it out alone.
host.credential_placeholder: Diploma in Tourism - Chef de Partie

about.title: What You'll Learn in the Webinar
about.description: A 2-hour live session covering everything you need to submit a strong Swiss YPV application — learn directly from a Young Professional Visa holder who has successfully secured an opportunity in Switzerland and guided applicants from non-European countries through the process.

points.eligibility_desc: Understand exactly which requirements apply to your country, age, education, and field
points.documents_desc: The exact paperwork Swiss authorities expect, in the order they want it
points.timing_desc: When to apply, when companies hire, and how to align your application
points.mistakes_desc: Real pitfalls that lead to delays or rejections, and how to sidestep them

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: What is the Young Professional Visa?
faq.a1: It's a government-supported trainee exchange program allowing young professionals from selected non-EU countries to gain work experience in Switzerland for up to 18 months, in their field of study.
faq.a2: Argentina, Australia, Canada, Chile, Indonesia, Japan, Monaco, New Zealand, Philippines, Russia, San Marino, South Africa, Tunisia, Ukraine, and USA. Each has specific age limits.
faq.q3: How much does the webinar cost?
faq.a3: The webinar is paid — exact pricing will be shown after you complete the free eligibility check. Pre-screening itself is free, takes 3 minutes, and is no-commitment.
faq.q4: What if I'm not eligible right now?
faq.a4: We'll let you know politely with the specific reason. Many applicants become eligible later as their circumstances change. There's no charge for the screening.
faq.q5: Is this a guaranteed visa service?
faq.a5: No. This is an educational webinar based on real experience — not a visa application service. The final decision rests with Swiss authorities. We help you understand the process and prepare a strong application.

cta_section.title: Ready to Find Out If You Qualify?

rejection.title: Unfortunately, You Don't Qualify at This Time
rejection.subtitle: Thank you for your interest in the Young Professional Visa Switzerland program.
rejection.country_not_eligible: Unfortunately, the Young Professional Visa Switzerland is only available to citizens of 15 specific countries through bilateral agreements. Citizens of {country} are not currently eligible for this program.
rejection.age_out_of_range: The Young Professional Visa Switzerland has an age requirement of {min}–{max} years for {country} passport holders. Based on your date of birth, you are currently {age} years old.
rejection.vocational_training_required: The program requires applicants to have completed vocational training or a university degree.
rejection.field_interest_required: The program requires applicants to be working in their field of study.
rejection.encouragement: Immigration programs evolve. If your circumstances change, we encourage you to re-apply in the future.
rejection.contact: If you believe this assessment is incorrect, please contact us.

eligible.lead: You are eligible to participate in the Young Professional Visa Switzerland program.
eligible.p1: This means you can already start applying for jobs in Switzerland on your own, as you meet the basic eligibility requirements for the program.
eligible.p2: You can begin applying directly to Swiss employers who are open to hiring international candidates under the Young Professional Visa. However, please note that securing a job offer is a required step before proceeding with the visa application.
eligible.p3: Make sure your CV and supporting documents are well-prepared and meet international standards, as the job market in Switzerland can be quite competitive.
eligible.p4: You can also check the official requirements and download the instructions <link>here</link>.
eligible.p5: To help you better understand the process and improve your chances of success, we offer a guidance service where you can gain practical insights into the Young Professional Visa and working in Switzerland.
eligible.p6: If you prefer structured guidance, step-by-step support, and insider tips, you can book a paid webinar session below.
eligible.disclaimer_p1: This webinar is intended to provide guidance and insights on the Young Professional Visa and working in Switzerland. It does not guarantee employment or visa approval and is not a job placement service.
eligible.disclaimer_p2: The content is based on personal experience. I am not a professional recruiter, but someone who wants to help others better understand the process of working abroad—especially in Switzerland.
eligible.disclaimer_p3: The fee is a form of appreciation for the time and knowledge shared during the session, not for selling job opportunities.
eligible.disclaimer_p4: By proceeding with the payment, you acknowledge and agree to these terms.
eligible.outro: Further details, including available session dates and payment instructions, will be shown once you select your preferred session.
eligible.closing: We look forward to supporting you on your journey to working in Switzerland.
eligible.disagree: No thanks, I'll explore on my own
eligible.agree: Yes, I'd like to join a webinar session
